mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate exceeding 4.5 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contractions in real GDP and retail sales indicate a broad demand shock that forces equity repricing, producing negative forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in quarterly return regressions or forward returns remain positive during joint demand contractions.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during supply normalization phases confirms capacity utilization improvements that boost short-term equity momentum over five trading days.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns are negative in over 55 percent of periods following positive INDPRO surprises during normalization regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings above 325 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 325 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm labor market tightening that supports corporate revenue growth and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint labor strength fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a rank correlation above 0.04 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect strong consumer confidence and construction sector activity, which transmit to positive short-term equity momentum over five trading days.
failure_condition: Five-day forward returns show no positive skew or rank IC below 0.01 following housing starts increases.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, rising federal funds rates coupled with elevated inflation expectations force rapid equity deleveraging, producing negative forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of tightening regime months despite rising rates and inflation.
